items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Strange, did Gilberto just vanish into thin air?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I see. A <ano=Flying Echo>Wingray</ano>, that explains it.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Wingray?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Yes, it's a type of Echo that can fly. People in Ragunna often use them to travel quickly between locations.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: If Gilberto set up this Wingray as an escape route, it should lead us straight to him.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Let's use the Grapple to hitch a ride on the Wingray.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Livia, Brenno! Any sign of Beppe?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Strange, where could Beppe be...?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Oh... Cut it out, Brenno! Stop that...

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Wait, Brenno's right here. Then who's poking me in the back? Beppe, you better have a good expla—

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: —Not Beppe. It's me. Abby!

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Eek!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: That's enough, Abby.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hic...! Lame. I was just trynna have some fun with her.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Oh, it's you. Pardon me, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: I thought you were at Ragunna City. Didn't expect to meet you here. Oh, uhh... Sorry, I don't think we've met.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Greetings, madam. My name is Zani. Averardo Bank.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: I don't think we've met before.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Well... It's probably because I haven't been to church much... Ahem. Please don't stare at me like that. There are more pressing matters.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: We're investigating the Echo incident from the Carnevale rehearsal at Whisperwind Haven, as requested by the Montelli family. One of our clues led us here.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: The Echo incident... Yes, I've also heard of it. I even heard you were caught right in the middle of it, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Please accept my apologies on behalf of the Order.

options.text: It's okay. I didn't get hurt.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: By the way, have any of you seen an Echo named Beppe? They're about this tall and usually wander around here, but I can't seem to find them anywhere...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: May I?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Sorry, we didn't see the Echo you mentioned, but we've found clues on their odd behavior. They all point to a member of the Fisalia family, Gilberto.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: He's also caused similar incidents here recently... Echoes went out of control and attacked each other.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: The Order has also received reports of missing Echoes in this area. Is it possible that the Fisalia family is involved? I find it really hard to believe.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: From what I know, the Fisalia family is deeply respectful of the Divinity. How could they commit such a blasphemous act? The Echoes are gifts from the Sentinel.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Have you seen this before?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: A petal? The ritual one? No, wait... this frequency...

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Oh, right! I think I saw this flower in the Cathedral of Mercury, and Priest Alessio was wearing it. Wait... Could he be involved with the Fisalia family's plans?

options.text: No, we're still lacking key evidence.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Imperator granted us Echoes, the skies, lands, oceans, and sustenance. To which we should all be grateful and reverent.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: I request to join your investigation, {PlayerName}. As a servant of the Sentinel, I simply cannot turn a blind eye to their unholy acts.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Fine by me. With someone from the Order as a neutral witness, the Fisalias won't be able to accuse us of fabricating evidence.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: As long as I'm not treated like a heretic and get kicked out of Rinascita later.

options.text: I'm okay with it as well.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Great, it seems we've reached an agreement.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Beppe? Thank the Divinity, you're safe! But what happened to you?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Wait! Beppe... Oh...

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Sorry, I got a bit carried away. Echoes don't usually act like this… Something must have startled them.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Something feels off. We should go check it out, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: Excellent. Everything is in place. Now, all we need is Her Majesty's approval.

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: O great Imperator. May you raise the tides, deliver your edict to our Queen, and let the clouds cover Ragunna, cleansing it of the wickedness left by the pleasure-seekers of this city of transgression.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Is he praying? I know the Fisalias are extremely devout, but who is this "Queen" he mentioned?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: This prayer sounds... odd. It's definitely not from the Order's Codex.

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: O Divinity in the Abyss, Lord of Tides and Riddles, may you soon be freed. Let all lies melt away under your power. May you grant me strength to overcome all who oppose you.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Lord of Tides and... Riddles?

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: Huh? Who's there!?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Uh-oh, things just went south. Looks like we've got a fight on our hands.

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: You must be that outlander, right? The news of your arrival is all over Ragunna...

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: Since you're here, I guess you've uncovered quite a bit... Though it's insignificant compared to the truth beneath the tides.

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: As a gesture of goodwill, let me offer a word of advice: Curiosity won't lead you to the truth you seek, but rather closer to—

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: —Your impending doom.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: He just turned Echoes into Tacet Discords. Is it the petals?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: You... What have you done to those Echoes!?

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: Merely the neccessary sacrifices for our one true Divinity.

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: No! This cannot be!

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: How pathetic. You're nothing but a discarded pawn.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: ...Which is not unusual with the Fisalia family.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Mr. Gilberto, please explain this "Lord of Tides and Riddles" you mentioned.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I appreciate your passion for the Order, Miss Phoebe, but now is not the time. We need to uncover the real culprit behind the Fisalias.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Oh, forgive me...

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: Do you really think you've won? No, it's already too late... Haha...

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: I might just be a pawn, insignificant and small, but while you're focusing on me, you've missed something far more important.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: He shows no remorse for his crimes. I'll contact Mr. Alberto and have the family send someone to take over.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: What? That's the... The Nimbus Sanctum.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: These Echoes... are converging towards the sea of clouds. What's happening?

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: Ha. Muahahaha! Magnificent! Lorelei is now ours for the taking... Just as the prophecy foretold! All shall be cleansed!

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: Now, let us wait in patience as the sea of clouds descends to engulf that corrupted city. Only the faithful will rise anew from the tides!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Lorelei?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: According to the Codex, Lorelei is a powerful Remnant creature residing in the Atrium of Reflections at the center of the Nimbus Sanctum.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Legends say the sea of clouds once flooded the Seminary of the Forgotten, reducing it to ruins, before spreading to Ragunna. It was Lorelei who curbed the raging clouds and ended the crisis.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: If... if they've really done something to Lorelei, then Mr. Gilberto wasn't just bluffing. The clouds might truly engulf Ragunna, leading to a disaster beyond our imagination.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: They are all mad... So this is what they've been planning all along.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: We have to do something.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: You two go ahead. I'll stay here and watch him until the family arrives, just in case he gets any bright ideas.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: I'll stay with you. After all, this could provoke outcry from the Fisalia family. If I testify about Gilberto's misdeeds on behalf of the Order, they can't deny the facts or exploit the situation.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I agree. But what about you, {PlayerName}...

options.text: Don't worry. I can take care of myself!

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: All right. There's a port nearby where you can catch a Gondola. It can take you close to the Atrium of Reflections.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: After we hand Gilberto over to the family, we'll meet up with you as soon as possible.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Please be extra careful, {PlayerName}! May the Sentinel protect you!
Aha. Perfect… There's still a Gondola here. I believe your name is… Noah, right?

items.speaker: Echo Boat
items.text: *Gentle sounds of approval*
It seems fate has brought us together once more. Don't you agree? Kind {Male=sir;Female=lady}.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You're…?
It seems you do remember me. That's right. We crossed paths at Madame Nyarla's mask shop.
Truth be told, I need to catch the final train in the Nimbus Sanctum today. However, this lone Gondola is all the harbor offers.
So… since we share the same destination, would you mind also sharing this Gondola with me?
Much obliged.
Well, then. Without further ado, let's be on our way.
It seems we have some time before the train arrives. How about a little chat to pass the time?
Well, then. Where shall we begin?
"The city, an abomination of chaos, claims its first death at birth. Its mortality has become our sustenance, like a whale that slowly sinks into the abyss."
This comment on Ragunna comes from Rosselli, a painter from the past. Tragically, he died in poverty, his work overlooked. Now, ironically, even a mere sketch of his commands over a million Credits.
Now. What is your impression of Ragunna so far?

options.text: A profit-driven place full of corruption.

items.text: Passionate... Indeed. Passion is a quality the Ragunnesi possess in abundance. It's also why the Carnevale is so eagerly anticipated.
Indeed. Ragunna is not as glamorous as it seems. Beneath the veneer of prosperity, avarice reigns supreme.
Indeed, faith is Ragunna's crown jewel. Our devotion to the Sentinel stands unrivaled throughout Rinascita.
A most prudent response. Perhaps you're still acquainting yourself with Ragunna, or you simply prefer discretion. Either way, I take no offense.
Ragunna has many facets. Most people only see one side of it, but for me—
It's like two sworn enemies are forcibly bound together. That's Ragunna—a city where tradition and innovation, openness and isolation, ceaselessly wrestle for dominance.
That is why the Montelli and Fisalia families are in a constant clash, as you may have seen.
The Montellis champion innovation, staking all on the Carnevale. Meanwhile, the Fisalias cling to tradition, colluding with the shadows that lurk in Ragunna's depths.
Fortunately, this long struggle behind the curtains will soon come to an end. Not because of the two families, but because of you.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Me?
Yes, you. The Fated One. Perhaps you're not merely entangled in Ragunna's conflict, but are, in fact, the very eye of the storm. The city's fate may well rest upon your shoulders.
You may have not noticed it yet, but we are... and have been in the same boat for a long time.
Ah. There's the train. It's time to say goodbye now.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I didn't catch your name.
I'll tell you next time. As for now, let's keep it a mystery.
Until we meet again, Fated One. May you find your own path in Ragunna.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: That flower... looks like the petals we found in Whisperwind Haven.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: It's the Fractsidus. I've dealt with them before, and I've seen a flower just like this.

items.speaker: Lorelei
items.text: How long have I been in slumber... This nightmare... How long was I trapped in it?

items.speaker: Lorelei
items.text: Are you also part of the dream?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: No, we are not... Might you be Lorelei?

items.speaker: Lorelei
items.text: I am. Travelers from afar, I sense… the kindness pulsing in your hearts. You must be the ones who broke the chains of my nightmare.

items.speaker: Lorelei
items.text: The oathbreakers, hiding behind their Masks of Madness… They tainted the Nimbus Sanctum with corrupted blooms, drowning me in rage and sorrow. Endless nightmares became my prison.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Masks of Madness... I recall those people at the entrance of the Atrium mentioned it.

items.speaker: Lorelei
items.text: Had you not freed me from my nightmare, I would never be able to fulfill the Sentinel's task… The tides of the Nimbus Sanctum would have engulfed the whole of Ragunna, just as they once claimed the sacred temple now lying in ruins beneath you.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Wait. The Sentinel? Does that mean...?

items.speaker: Lorelei
items.text: Yes, the Sentinel molded me from a mindless being to the form you see, and bestowed upon me the most precious gift... A soul.

items.speaker: Lorelei
items.text: They commanded me to restrain the clouds and prevent the Remnant Energy within it from... dissipating.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Can you contact Imperator? I need to meet with the Sentinel.

items.speaker: Lorelei
items.text: Unfortunately, it lies beyond my power. The Sentinel's link to the mortal realm is severed. Only through collective prayer, and a rite, at the height of the Carnevale, only then can mortals briefly commune with Them.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: A rite... You mean the Carnevale?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: No, that's impossible! The current Primus, Fenrico the Enlightened One, was given a direct oracle from the Sentinel. The Sentinel has always been with the people!

items.speaker: Lorelei
items.text: I'm afraid the Sentinel's current state... transcends mortal understanding. Your Primus is no exception.

items.speaker: Lorelei
items.text: Should doubt linger in your mind, I implore you to await the rite's completion. The Sentinel will surely answer the people's call—not your so-called… Enlightened One.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: One last thing. Those oathbreakers wearing Masks of Madness you mentioned, do you know who they are?

items.speaker: Lorelei
items.text: All that I know is that they are not from Rinascita.

items.speaker: Lorelei
items.text: In the whispers between dreams, I heard them speak of their leader, a woman they called…

items.speaker: Lorelei
items.text: "—the Conductor."

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Conductor...

items.speaker: Lorelei
items.text: It is time I return to my slumber. Their wicked acts have scarred the sea of clouds, and I must mend it… within my dreams.

items.speaker: Lorelei
items.text: The song shall guide you out of this domain. Farewell, visitors.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: What a wild turn this job's taken… Seeing the legendary Lorelei up close? Almost makes it worth the trouble.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Hmm? Are you all right, Phoebe?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Huh? I-I'm fine. I'm just… processing Lorelei's words. Forgive me, I… I need a moment to reflect.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: In any case, we managed to stop their plot in time and found the missing Vitreum Dancer.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Speaking of that Vitreum Dancer… What brought it here? Lorelei's song, or maybe the Sentinel's guidance?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: But those wearing the Masks of Madness... The Fractsidus, right? I'm curious about their role in all this. I think they supplied Gilberto with the flowers to turn Echoes into Tacet Discords.

options.text: Yeah, the Fractsidus is behind this.
Their actions remind me of something...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: What's our next move, {PlayerName}?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: You're right... Those snakes lurked in the shadows, using Gilberto as their fall guy. We can't expose them just by ourselves.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Oh, another thing. Since the Fractsidus is conspiring with the Fisalias, that makes them an enemy of the Montelli family.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: There happens to be an auction at Averardo Vault today. The top members of the family will be there.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: We should tip off the family about the Fractsidus' scheme. The Montellis can't afford to ignore this threat.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: What about you, Phoebe?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: I intend to return to the Order first. While I believe Lorelei is truthful, I can't imagine the Primus would deceive our people. He is, after all, the <ano=Sentinel's Resonator>Enlightened One</ano>.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: According to Lorelei, the Sentinel will deliver an oracle during the Carnevale. That's when the truth will come to light.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Speaking of which, are all Primuses called the <ano=Sentinel's Resonator>Enlightened One</ano>?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: No, only Lord Fenrico has ever directly communicated with the Sentinel. Previous Primuses were merely indirect representatives, not the Enlightened One...

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Silence and composure are gifts from the great Imperator. I'll keep today's events between us, locked away in my heart.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Then I believe this is where we part ways.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: The Codex reminds us, "It is not the lighthouse that guides us through the storm to safety, but our own eyes fixed on the shore."

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: I'd like to offer these words to you—may your eyes always shine bright with clarity.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I guess "singing" is our only option for clearing that barrier.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: But which song?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The stela we saw mentioned the Sentinel, and we're in the ruins of the seminary.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Meaning it should be a "chorale"? There are also the inscription in front of the barrier: "We mourn the martyrdom of the Blessed Maiden."

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Any chorales you know ring a bell, Phoebe?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: The martyrdom of the Blessed Maiden... Could this be the "Aria of the Blessed Maiden"? It's a chorale rarely used these days. I doubt most Priests even know of it.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The Blessed Maiden? We met a playwright named Cristoforo earlier. He shared some stories about her.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Phoebe, if you know this chorale, why not give it a try? It might just clear the barrier.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: M-me?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: O-okay then. I suppose it's our best option at the moment. I'll give it a try. Ahem...
